rewordings.q: Two residues join and shed a molecule of water; name the covalent link that results.
Chymotrypsin cleaves which backbone linkage between adjacent residues in a chain?
At a pH well above its pI, does a free amino acid carry net positive or net negative charge?
Below the isoelectric point, what is the sign of the overall charge on a zwitterionic residue?
During oxygen unloading in active muscle, does hemoglobin favor its tense or relaxed conformation?
A rightward shift of the oxygen-binding curve reflects lower affinity — which subunit state dominates?
An enzyme lowers what quantity along the reaction coordinate to speed a reaction?
Does a catalyst change the free-energy difference between products and reactants?
A competitive inhibitor changes the apparent value of which Michaelis-Menten parameter?
With saturating substrate, can a purely competitive inhibitor still lower the maximum turnover rate?
Net phosphorylation of ADP per glucose during substrate-level glycolysis totals how many?
Ignoring the electron transport chain, how many high-energy phosphate bonds does one glucose net in the cytosol?
Which membrane hosts the protein complexes that pump protons during aerobic respiration?
The proton-motive force is built across which specific boundary of the mitochondrion?
Reading the template strand 3'-to-5', RNA polymerase builds the transcript in which direction?
Which end of a growing mRNA gets the newest nucleotide added?
Moving a 2-coulomb charge across a 4-volt drop expends how many joules?
Express the energy to shift charge q through potential difference V.
Doubling the separation between two point charges scales the Coulomb force by what factor?
If distance triples, the electrostatic force between two charges becomes what fraction of the original?
A converging lens forms an image on the far side; is that image real or virtual?
For an object inside the focal length of a magnifying lens, what kind of image appears?
As a gas is compressed at constant temperature, how does its pressure respond?
Holding temperature fixed, halving a gas's volume multiplies its pressure by what?
Adding heat while a substance melts changes which property: temperature or phase?
During a phase transition at constant pressure, what happens to the temperature reading?
A weak acid's conjugate base is described as strong or weak relative to it?
If an acid ionizes only slightly, how does its conjugate base's tendency to accept protons compare?
A block slides at constant velocity down an incline; what is the net force on it?
When kinetic friction exactly balances gravity's component along a ramp, what is the acceleration?
A pendulum's period depends on which two quantities?
Does swinging a pendulum with a heavier bob change how long each swing takes?
After conditioning, the bell that once meant nothing now triggers salivation; label that bell.
In Pavlov's setup, the formerly neutral cue becomes which kind of stimulus once learning occurs?
Rewarding a behavior on an unpredictable schedule makes it especially resistant to what?
Which reinforcement schedule yields the steadiest, extinction-resistant responding?
Attributing a stranger's rude act to their character rather than their situation is which bias?
Overweighting disposition and underweighting context when judging others names what error?
Encoding, storage, and which third process complete the basic memory model?
Pulling a stored memory back into awareness is called what stage?
Which brain structure is most tied to consolidating new declarative memories?
Bilateral damage here blocks forming new explicit memories — name it.
A survey that measures what it claims to measure is said to have which property?
Consistency of a test's results across repeated administrations is termed what?
A rise in a population's median income most directly shifts which stratification measure?
Which concept captures the unequal distribution of wealth across a society?
An author's unstated assumption that a passage's argument rests on is best called its what?
Identifying the claim a paragraph is built to support means finding its what?
A passage that concedes an opposing view before rebutting it is using which rhetorical move?
When a writer raises a counterargument only to dismantle it, that device is named what?
A tone that is gently mocking while appearing sincere is best described how?
Which single word best captures an author who undercuts praise with subtle irony?
Applying a passage's principle to a brand-new scenario tests which reasoning skill?
Extending the author's logic beyond the text is which category of CARS question?
Splicing removes which non-coding segments from a primary transcript?
The stretches retained in mature mRNA after splicing are called what?
A missense mutation changes a codon so that it specifies what?
Which point mutation converts a sense codon into a premature stop signal?
In a series circuit, how does total resistance compare to the largest single resistor?
Adding resistors in parallel drives the equivalent resistance in which direction?
